LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT EXTREME AUTO RECOVERY, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FALSE IMPRISONMENT, ASSAULT AND BATTERY, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND VIOLATION OF UNRUH ACT BECAUSE THE CLAIMS ARE BASED UPON MR. JONES' ALLEGEDLY FALSE REPORT TO THE POLICE. MR. JONES' REPORT TO THE POLICE IS ABSOLUTELY PRIVILEGED UNDER CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE SECTION 47(B), AND CANNOT BE THE BASIS FOR THE TORTS ALLEGED IN THIS CASE. HAGBERG V. CAL. FED BANK FSB (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 350, 364. FURTHER, DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE ASSAULT AND BATTERY CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF ADMITS THAT MR. JONES DID NOT ENGAGE IN SUCH CONDUCT AND PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY HOLDING THAT DEFENDANT CAN BE LIABLE FOR POLICE MISCONDUCT. DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE NEGLIGENT HIRING CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THERE IS NO PROOF THAT MR. JONES COMMITTED AN ACTIONABLE UNDERLYING TORT. THE REQUEST TO DENY THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(I) OR TO ORDER THE DEFENDANT TO PRODUCE MR. JONES FOR DEPOSITION OR OTHER DISCOVERY IS DENIED. THE COURT ASSUMES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS REFERRING TO CCP 437C(H). PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MADE THE REQUIRED SHOWING THAT FACTS ESSENTIAL TO JUSTIFY OPPOSITION MAY EXIST, BUT COULD NOT BE PRESENTED IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION REGARDING THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT HIRING, RETENTION AND SUPERVISION. IN FACT, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION SUGGESTS THAT MR. JONES WOULD NOT BE AVAILABLE TO BE A WITNESS AT ALL. (IBRAHIM DECL. 9.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT DANIEL JONES' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings/Alt Motion For Entry Of Judgment In Favor Of Daniel Jones. The Court grants defendant's motion and enters judgment in defendant's favor against plaintiff. Judicially noticeable documents show that (1) the Court granted summary adjudication on all of the causes of action alleged against defendant in the operative amended complaint; (order granted summary adjudication filed on 9/16/13); (2) the Court gave plaintiff leave to attempt to plead a cause of action for extortion; (3) the Court denied plaintiff's motion to amend (minute order denying amendment filed on 9/6/13); and (4) the order denying amendment denied the motion; the order did not deny the motion without prejudice. Therefore, plaintiff has no viable claims against defendant and defendant is entitled to a judgment of dismissal. See CCP 581(m). Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Saeed Ibrahim, 415.448.7419, plaintiff in pro per.
Cynthia M. Soliman, Esq., 415.362.7126, for defendant Extreme Auto Recovery Company.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT EXTREME AUTO RECOVERY, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish the causes of action for false imprisonment, assault and battery, intentional infliction of emotional distress, negligent infliction of emotional distress, and violation of Unruh Act because the claims are based upon Mr. Jones' allegedly false report to the police. Mr. Jones' report to the police is absolutely privileged under Civil Procedure Code Section 47(b), and cannot be the basis for the torts alleged in this case. Hagberg v. Cal. Fed Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350, 364. Further, Defendant is entitled to summary adjudication on the assault and battery cause of action because plaintiff admits that Mr. Jones did not engage in such conduct and Plaintiff cites no authority holding that defendant can be liable for police misconduct. Defendant is entitled to summary adjudication on the negligent hiring cause of action because there is no proof that Mr. Jones committed an actionable underlying tort. The request to deny the motion for summary judgment pursuant to CCP 437c(i) or to order the Defendant to produce Mr. Jones for deposition or other discovery is denied. The Court assumes that Plaintiff was referring to CCP 437c(h). Plaintiff has not made the required showing that facts essential to justify opposition may exist, but could not be presented in opposition to the motion regarding the cause of action for negligent hiring, retention and supervision. In fact, Plaintiff's declaration suggests that Mr. Jones would not be available to be a witness at all. (Ibrahim Decl. 9.) Order signed in open Court.
